texto: Life and study have persuaded me of the openness of history. There is no inevitability in history. Thinking about what might have happened, what could have happened, is a necessary element in trying to understand what did happen. And if, as I believe, individual acts of decency and courage make a difference, then they need to be recorded and remembered.
explicacao: Citação de Fritz Stern sobre enfrentar incertezas com ação. “Life and study have persuaded me of the openness of history. There is no inevitability in history…” articula reflexao, history, happened em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como estudos e reflexao.
